title: rubus fruticosus subsp. koehleri
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. koehleri, also known as the bramble or blackberry, is a subspecies of the blackberry plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. koehleri include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, digestive issues, and menstrual cramps.
